REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, OTHER TERRY MOLLICA (NON-PARTY), GLEN OLIVES MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF PRIOR ORDER; IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO QUASH IS SET FOR HEARING ON JANUARY 22, 2026. IT SHOULD NOT HAVE BEEN TAKEN OFF SEPTEMBER 12, 2025 CALENDAR AND SHOULD HAVE BEEN CONTINUED TO BE HEARD WITH THE MOTION TO COMPEL TURNOVER OF FUNDS. MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. MOTION TO COMPEL TURNOVER OF FUNDS WAS PERSONALLY SERVED ON MR. OLIVES AND SUB-SERVED ON MR. MOLLICA PER THE PROOFS OF SERVICE FILED ON SEPTEMBER 30, 2025. THE COURT NOTES THAT BY THE TIME THE MOTION TO COMPEL TURNOVER WAS HEARD ON NOVEMBER 19, 2025, MOTION TO QUASH WAS STALE AS IT WAS FILED MONTHS BEFORE THE SERVICE REFLECTED IN THE SEPTEMBER 30, 2025 PROOFS OF SERVICE. ANY ISSUES WITH JURISDICTION COULD HAVE BEEN AND WERE ADDRESSED IN THE OCTOBER 27, 2025 OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL TURNOVER OF FUNDS. THOSE ARGUMENTS HAVE BEEN CONSIDERED IN THE COURT'S RULING. MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER ON THE MOTION TO COMPEL TURNOVER OF FUNDS IS DENIED.PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B).JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Hearing Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice In Support; Declaration Of Glen H. Olives In Support Of Motion (Motions)
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

OTHER TERRY MOLLICA (NON-PARTY), GLEN OLIVES Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order;

is GRANTED as follows: Motion to Quash is set for hearing on January 22, 2026. It should not have been taken off September 12, 2025 calendar and should have been continued to be heard with the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds.
Motion to Quash is Denied. Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds was personally served on Mr. Olives and sub-served on Mr. Mollica per the proofs of service filed on September 30, 2025. The Court notes that by the time the Motion to Compel Turnover was heard on November 19, 2025, Motion to Quash was stale as it was filed months before the service reflected in the September 30, 2025 proofs of service. Any issues with jurisdiction could have been and were addressed in the October 27, 2025 Opposition to the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds. Those arguments have been considered in the Court's ruling.
Motion for Reconsideration of the order on the Motion to Compel Turnover of Funds is Denied.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Hearing Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice In Support; Declaration Of Glen H. Olives In Support Of Motion (Motions)
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Continued To Jan-22-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

OTHER TERRY MOLLICA (NON-PARTY), GLEN OLIVES Motion For Reconsideration Of Prior Order;

is continued to January 22, 2026 on the Court's own motion.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Receiver'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Turn Over Of Amounts Held In Client Trust Account
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Alan Feld, Esq. for Plaintiff
Terry Molica, in pro per, non-party
Glen Olives, in pro per, non-party
Angela Butcher, Esq. for Receiver Greg Williams

Hearing held.
Mr. Mollica disputes the minutes from the Sept. 12 hearing wherein the court had recused itself.
Mr. Olives supplements Mr. Mollica's statements.

The court inquires as to the funds held in the client's trust account.

Mr. Mollica offers proof to the court's query.

Discussion and argument among parties re: funds in the client's trust account, agreement on the funds in the client's trust account.

The court hears arguments from all parties present.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

OTHER GREGG WILLIAMS Receiver's Motion To Compel Turn Over Of Amounts Held In Client Trust Account;

is GRANTED. Sums at issue on this motion held in the Trust account for 644 Broadway LLC shall be turned over to the court appointment receiver within 10 day of notice of entry of this order served on counsel holding the funds in the trust account.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order.

Joinder Of Non-Party Receiver'S Motion To Compel Turnover Of Amounts Held In Client Trust Account
Off Calendar Nov-19-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Alan Feld, Esq. for Plaintiff
Terry Molica, in pro per, non-party
Glen Olives, in pro per, non-party
Angela Butcher, Esq. for Receiver Greg Williams

Hearing held.
Mr. Mollica disputes the minutes from the Sept. 12 hearing wherein the court had recused itself.
Mr. Olives supplements Mr. Mollica's statements.

The court inquires as to the funds held in the client's trust account.

Mr. Mollica offers proof to the court's query.

Discussion and argument among parties re: funds in the client's trust account, agreement on the funds in the client's trust account.

The court hears arguments from all parties present.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF Joinder Of Non-Party Receiver'S Motion To Compel Turnover Of Amounts Held In Client Trust Account;

is GRANTED. Sums at issue on this motion held in the Trust account for 644 Broadway LLC shall be turned over to the court appointment receiver within 10 day of notice of entry of this order served on counsel holding the funds in the trust account.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order.
